zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Weston於大約1270年出生在英國武斯特斯爾洛可莫頓時，她的父親Richard de Weston15歲，母親Joan Eleanore Hatch31歲。她於1290年與Robert de Throckmorton III在英國斯塔福爾牛頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1315年在她的家鄉享年46歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Weston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of numerous places called Weston, from Old English west ‘west’ + tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’.

topographic name from Middle English atte west tun(e) ‘at the west farmstead, settlement’ (Old English west + tūn), or from Middle English (bi) weste(n)tune (Old English be westan tūne or be west in tūne), denoting someone who lived either to the west of a settlement or in the western part of a settlement. The latter was a common surname in Sussex.

variants.name: Wesson
meanings: English (Midlands and Kent): variant of   Weston  .
History: John Wesson came from England to Salem, MA, in 1644.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Weston was born about 1270, in Throckmorton, Worcestershire, England, her father, Richard de Weston, was 15 and her mother, Joan Eleanore Hatch, was 31. She married Robert de Throckmorton III in 1290, in Newton, Staffordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1315, in her hometown, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Weston est née environ 1270, à Throckmorton, Worcestershire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Weston, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Joan Eleanore Hatch, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Robert de Throckmorton III en 1290, à Newton, Staffordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1315, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 46 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Weston(이)가 대략 1270년 영국 워스스터실르 스록모르턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard de Weston(은)는 15세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Eleanore Hatch(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1290년 영국 스태퍼드실르 뉴턴에서 Robert de Throckmorton III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1315년 그녀의 고향에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Robert de Throckmorton III em 1290, em Newton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1315, em sua cidade natal, com 46 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Weston родилась около 1270 г. в местности троккмортон, ворсестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard de Weston было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Eleanore Hatch, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert de Throckmorton III в 1290 г. в местности нютон, стаффордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1315 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 46 лет.
